tiny_practice.action: Take 30 seconds to reflect on how you can generously support your local church or fellow believers this week

two_step.reflect_question: How can I intentionally practice generosity and support for my spiritual community?

options.response: Set up an automatic monthly donation to your church or a Christian mission organization
Volunteer for a ministry or find a practical way to serve church members in need
Commit to praying regularly for church leaders and missionaries

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a picture of how different people in a church community can support each other, using symbols or images representing various forms of help

reevaluation.question: How has reading this chapter changed your perspective on Christian community and support?
options: I see giving as more of a spiritual discipline now
I understand the importance of supporting other believers
I feel challenged to be more intentional about generosity
I recognize the global nature of the church community

three_choice.question: Which aspect of Christian community challenges you most?

choices.expansion: Intentional, planned giving transforms charity from sporadic emotion to disciplined spiritual practice.
Authentic Christian relationships require transparency and mutual spiritual endorsement.
Kingdom work transcends individual congregations, requiring strategic partnership.

socratic_nudge.question: How might your personal prosperity be a stewardship opportunity rather than a personal entitlement?

moral_slider.question: Where do you balance personal resources and communal needs?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of Christian giving evolved?
options: I now see giving as spiritual discipline
Community needs matter more than personal comfort
Generosity is an act of worship
Resources are ultimately God's, not mine

thematic_summaries.title: Christian Generosity and Supporting the Church
thematic_summaries.summary: Paul instructs the Corinthians about systematic, proportional giving to support the Jerusalem church during a time of economic hardship. The collection demonstrates Christian unity, compassion, and practical love across different congregations.

thematic_summaries.title: Apostolic Leadership and Church Cooperation
thematic_summaries.summary: Paul models collaborative ministry by coordinating travel plans, recommending trusted co-workers, and maintaining relational accountability between churches. His approach emphasizes interdependence and mutual support in gospel mission.

reevaluation.question: How should modern churches understand financial giving and missional support?
options: Giving should be proportional to individual prosperity
Financial support demonstrates spiritual connection between congregations
Missional giving transcends local church boundaries
Transparency and accountability are crucial in financial partnerships
